name: uems-agent-chat
description: Chat agents for UEMS Native Agent development — architecture, planning, development, review, and documentation

uems-agent-chat.syncIntervalHours.description: How often (in hours) to sync agent files from the remote repository. Set to 0.5 for 30 minutes.

uems-agent-chat.autoSync.description: Automatically sync agent files on activation and periodically.

uems-agent-chat.autoUpdate.description: Automatically check for and install extension updates from the remote repository.

uems-agent-chat.devMode.description: Development mode: use the local uems-ai-toolkit workspace folder for agents and guidelines instead of syncing from master. Enable this when developing on a feature branch.

uems-agent-chat.devRepoPath.description: Path to the local uems-ai-toolkit repo root (used when devMode is true). Auto-detected from workspace folders if empty.

uems-agent-chat.httpBridge.enabled.description: Start an HTTP server that exposes the UEMS agent orchestrator for external frontends.

uems-agent-chat.httpBridge.port.description: Port for the HTTP bridge server.

chatParticipants.name: UEMS-Agent
chatParticipants.description: Explore UEMS native agent repositories — codebase, dependencies, architecture, and conventions

disambiguation.description: Explore UEMS native agent codebase, repo structure, dependencies, and conventions
examples: What repos does patch-management depend on?
Find the networking wrapper in agent-utils

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with UEMS Agents
walkthroughs.description: Verify prerequisites and start exploring UEMS native agent repositories with AI.

steps.title: Check Prerequisites
steps.description:
Verify that Git and GitHub Copilot Chat are available on your system.

[Check Prerequisites](command:uems-agent-chat.checkPrerequisites)

steps.title: Setup Workspace
steps.description:
Clone the UEMS agent repositories for your platform so agents can search and navigate code.

[macOS](command:uems-agent-chat.setupWorkspace?%7B%22platform%22%3A%22mac%22%7D) · [Linux](command:uems-agent-chat.setupWorkspace?%7B%22platform%22%3A%22linux%22%7D) · [Windows](command:uems-agent-chat.setupWorkspace?%7B%22platform%22%3A%22windows%22%7D)

steps.title: Start Exploring
steps.description:
Open Copilot Chat with the UEMS Agent Explorer to search code, trace dependencies, and understand the architecture.

[Open UEMS Agent Explorer](command:uems-agent-chat.openChat)

languageModelTools.name: uems_agent_search_repos
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search for code patterns across UEMS agent repositories. Returns file paths, line numbers, and matching text. Supports multiple search terms separated by spaces, pipes (|), or ampersands (&) — each term is matched independently (OR logic). Set isRegex=true only for advanced regex patterns (character classes, quantifiers, lookaheads). Use this to find where a class, function, macro, string, or pattern is defined or used across repos.

query.description: Search pattern. Multiple terms can be separated by spaces, pipes (|), or ampersands (&) to match any of them (OR logic). Examples: 'WriteEvent ERRMSG' finds both, 'NSXPCConnection' finds exact match, 'init.*Logger' with isRegex=true for regex.

repos.description: Optional list of repo names to search (e.g. ['agent-utils', 'patch-management']). Searches all workspace repos if omitted.

filePattern.description: Optional glob pattern to filter files (e.g. '*.swift', '*.{h,m}').

isRegex.description: Set to true only for advanced regex syntax (character classes, quantifiers, lookaheads). NOT needed for multi-term searches — spaces, pipes, and ampersands are automatically handled as OR separators. Default: false.

languageModelTools.name: uems_agent_list_components
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List native code components (classes, protocols, structs, enums, functions, XPC services, Go interfaces) defined in a UEMS agent repository. Supports Swift, Objective-C, C, Go, and C++. Use this to discover what components exist in a repo.

platform.description: Target platform — determines file extensions and patterns. Use 'cross-platform' to scan all native languages (Go, C, Swift, ObjC). Default: 'mac'.

languageModelTools.name: uems_agent_find_wrapper
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Find Agent-Utils wrappers for a given capability such as networking, file operations, process launching, logging, secure storage, XPC, or crypto. Use this before writing code that touches system APIs to check if a wrapper already exists.

capability.description: The capability to search for — e.g. 'networking', 'file', 'process', 'logging', 'secure-storage', 'xpc', 'crypto'.

repo.description: Repo name to search for wrappers in. Default: 'agent-utils'.

platform.description: Target platform — determines search keywords and file extensions. Default: 'mac'.

languageModelTools.name: uems_agent_dependency_graph
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the dependency graph for a specific UEMS repo or the full platform. Shows upstream dependencies and downstream dependents organized by layer (0-4). Use this to understand cross-repo impact before making changes.

repo.description: Repo name to get dependencies for. If omitted, returns the full platform graph.

direction.description: 'up' for upstream deps, 'down' for downstream dependents, 'both' for full graph. Default: 'both'.

languageModelTools.name: uems_agent_validate_tag
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Validate a git tag format against UEMS convention (PRODUCTNAME_YY.MM.BUILD) and check if it exists in a repo. Returns format validity, existence status, and similar tag suggestions if the tag is not found.

repo.description: Repository name to check the tag in.

languageModelTools.name: uems_agent_create_branch
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create a git branch from a tag or base branch across one or more UEMS repos. Validates branch naming convention (feature/, bugfix/, hotfix/, release/) and tag format before creating. Use this during environment setup to create working branches.

repos.description: List of repo names to create the branch in.

branchName.description: Branch name following convention: feature/<topic>, bugfix/<topic>, hotfix/<topic>, release/<version>.

fromRef.description: Tag or branch to create from (e.g. 'UEMS_MAC_AGENT_UTILS_26.05.01' or 'master').

languageModelTools.name: uems_agent_setup_workspace
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Clone or fetch multiple UEMS agent repositories and verify workspace readiness. Optionally checkout a specific branch in all repos. Reports clone/fetch status, current branch, and clean/dirty state for each repo.

repos.description: Specific repo names to set up. If omitted, sets up all repos for the platform.

targetDir.description: Base directory to clone repos into. Defaults to first workspace folder.

branch.description: Branch to checkout after clone/fetch. Skipped for repos where the branch doesn't exist. If omitted, repos stay on their current branch.

languageModelTools.name: uems_agent_load_guidelines
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Load UEMS engineering guidelines, security standards, coding conventions, doc-standards, and review-standards from the synced store. Returns file contents organized by category. Use this instead of cloning the uems-ai-toolkit repo — guidelines are synced automatically by the extension.

platform.description: Target platform for platform-specific guidelines. Default: 'mac'.

category.description: Which guidelines to load — 'common' for shared standards, 'platform' for platform-specific, 'doc-standards' for documentation standards, 'review-standards' for delta review report template and standards, 'all' for everything. Default: 'all'.

files.description: Optional list of specific filenames to load (e.g. ['grounding-rules.md', 'repo-map.md']). If omitted, loads all files in the category.

languageModelTools.name: uems_agent_load_skills
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Load reusable skill procedures (SKILL.md files) from the synced skills store. Skills are focused, agent-specific procedures for tasks like checkpoint management, symbol tracing, and platform confirmation. Pass specific skill names via 'files' to load only what you need, or omit to load all skills.

files.description: Skill names to load (e.g. ['tool-preference-rules', 'checkpoint-management']). Each name is a skill directory containing a SKILL.md file. If omitted, loads all available skills.

languageModelTools.name: uems_agent_diff_branches
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the unified diff and per-file stats between two git branches across one or more UEMS repos. Returns changed file list, insertion/deletion counts, and the diff output. Use this for delta code reviews.

repos.description: Optional list of repo names. If omitted, diffs all workspace repos.

sourceBranch.description: The source branch containing the changes (e.g. 'feature/new-feature').

targetBranch.description: The target/base branch to compare against (e.g. 'master').

files.description: Optional list of file paths to scope the diff to. Use this to batch large diffs by requesting specific files per call.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run copy-assets && npm run package
scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.build:vsix: npm run copy-assets && npx @vscode/vsce package -o releases/uems-agent-chat.vsix
